deep_dive.title: Convex Relaxations for the Optimization of Markov Processes
deep_dive.abstract: In this paper, we study the problem of optimizing Markov processes that interpolate between two prescribed probability distributions while minimizing a given cost. The main computational challenge is the curse of dimensionality: in high-dimensional state spaces, representing the full distribution is intractable. To address this, we reformulate the problem in terms of sequential couplings and develop convex relaxations based on local marginals and cluster moments. These relaxations exploit locality and sparse interaction structure, provide computable lower bounds, and recover low-order statistics of the intermediate laws. We identify dynamic optimal transport as a special case of our Markov process optimization problem and develop a procedure for recovering the underlying Benamou--Brenier dynamics from the relaxed solution. We also show that the procedure extends to more general Markov processes and illustrate it with a constrained process between Ising models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: optimizing Markov processes that interpolate between two prescribed distributions, dynamic optimal transport
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 优化插值两个指定概率分布的马尔可夫过程，涉及动态最优传输
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 优化插值两个指定概率分布的马尔可夫过程，涉及动态最优传输
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 优化插值两个指定概率分布的马尔可夫过程，涉及动态最优传输
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes convex relaxations for optimizing Markov processes interpolating two distributions, linking to dynamic optimal transport, providing computable lower bounds and recovering low-order statistics.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对在两个指定概率分布之间插值的马尔可夫过程优化问题，提出了基于局部边际和簇矩的凸松弛方法，有效克服了高维状态空间的维数灾难。方法将动态最优传输作为特例，并给出了可计算的下界，能够恢复中间分布的低阶统计量。理论分析与数值实验验证了该方法的可扩展性和有效性，为扩散桥的构建和最优传输提供了新的工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对在两个指定概率分布之间插值的马尔可夫过程优化问题，提出了基于局部边际和簇矩的凸松弛方法，有效克服了高维状态空间的维数灾难。方法将动态最优传输作为特例，并给出了可计算的下界，能够恢复中间分布的低阶统计量。理论分析与数值实验验证了该方法的可扩展性和有效性，为扩散桥的构建和最优传输提供了新的工具。
